_comment: Best-effort catalog of laptop mfg+model combos where the OEM left an S3 (traditional sleep) opt-in in the BIOS, letting the user avoid Modern Standby (S0ix) entirely. The power module normally branches off `powercfg /a` output — if S3 is unavailable at runtime, it defaults to 'hibernate on timer, never sleep' (see knowledge_base/session_2026-07-04_lenovo_slim7prox.md problem 3 for the Ryzen 6000 wake-freeze pattern). This file is a hint layer: if the machine matches, the module can suggest 'flip the BIOS setting <X> to enable Windows S3 and use traditional sleep instead of hibernate-on-timer.' ALWAYS re-check `powercfg /a` after the user reboots — this list is not authoritative.
_bestEffort: Small and volatile. OEMs remove S3 opt-ins across BIOS revisions without announcement. Match is a hint, not a guarantee. Always defer to `powercfg /a` runtime output before applying any change.
_matchRule: mfg matches Win32_ComputerSystem.Manufacturer exactly (case-insensitive). modelPattern is a regex against Win32_ComputerSystem.Model. Both must match for an entry to apply.

entries.notes: Ryzen 4000-era Ideapad. BIOS lets you flip between Windows 10 (S0ix / Modern Standby) and Linux (S3). Switching to Linux gives you S3 in Windows too.
Ryzen 5000 Ideapad refresh — many SKUs kept the toggle.
entries.verify: Some BIOS revisions removed the toggle in updates

entries.notes: Ryzen ThinkPads through gen 3 broadly retained the S3 toggle. Later gens (4+) increasingly locked to Modern Standby only.

entries.biosMenuPath: Advanced > Built-In Device Options > S3 Sleep
entries.notes: HP business laptops from the G6-G8 range often kept S3 selectable via BIOS. G9+ mostly Modern Standby only.
entries.verify: BIOS revision matters — HP occasionally removes on major updates

entries.biosMenuPath: Power Management > Block Sleep — off; then Sleep = S3
entries.notes: Dell Latitude AMD SKUs (Ryzen 4000/5000-era) had S3 as an alternative to Modern Standby via BIOS. Newer Intel Latitude 14th-gen removed the toggle.
entries.verify: Dell BIOS labeling varies by generation

entries.notes: ASUS mostly does NOT expose an S3 toggle on modern AMD/Intel laptops. Included here to explicitly flag: don't tell the user to look for a BIOS setting that isn't there.
entries.verify: Some pre-2022 SKUs did have it
